summary: Multi-tenant Phase 1 backend regression validated. 16/16 tests pass. super_admin (gutenberg@sigesc.com) successfully accesses all listing endpoints with no 403 regression and POST /api/schools persists mantenedora_id correctly.

minor.issue: School Pydantic response model does NOT expose `mantenedora_id`. The field IS persisted in MongoDB (verified directly via DB query) but is hidden from the API response. Frontend cannot read which tenant a school belongs to without an extra lookup. Recommend adding `mantenedora_id: Optional[str] = None` to the School model.

test_results_by_feature.auth_login_super_admin: PASS - login returns role=super_admin and mantenedora_id=multi-tenant-ui-5
test_results_by_feature.schools_list: PASS - returns ESCOLA TESTE MULTISSERIADA (no longer empty)
test_results_by_feature.create_school_tenant_injection: PASS - With X-Mantenedora-Id header, POST /api/schools persists mantenedora_id (verified in MongoDB). Without header, super_admin gets 400 'Selecione uma mantenedora antes de criar a escola' which is the intended cross-tenant guard.

critical_code_review_comments: [MINOR] backend/routers/schools.py create_school returns the Pydantic School object directly (line 56: `return school_obj`), which does NOT carry the mantenedora_id field even though it was added to the dict that was inserted. Same gap on GET. Add `mantenedora_id: Optional[str] = None` to the School model in models.py so the API response exposes it. Without this, frontend cannot tell which tenant a school belongs to.

[INFO] Behavior of POST /api/schools requires super_admin to set X-Mantenedora-Id header (or ?mantenedora_id=) — without it, returns 400. This is the intended guard per tenant_scope.py, but the frontend MUST send the header (or the user must select a tenant context) before creating schools. Document this for the frontend team.

[INFO] auth_middleware.require_roles correctly fast-paths super_admin (line 76-77) and treats gerente as admin (line 87-88). check_school_access (line 140) and get_user_permissions (line 217) both include super_admin/gerente. Looks correct.

[INFO] tenant_scope.py is well-documented and only currently wired in routers/schools.py; the docstring lists 16+ collections that still need scoping in Phase 2 (students, staff, classes, courses, enrollments, grades, etc.). Cross-tenant data leakage is possible today for non-super_admin users in a multi-tenant scenario, but for the current single-mantenedora seed (only one mantenedora exists) this is not yet a runtime issue.

[INFO] For Phase 2, consider also wrapping list_schools cache key by tenant_id so that switching mantenedora context doesn't return stale cached results across tenants — already done correctly (line 71 includes tenant in cache_params).
seed_data_creation: Created and removed TEST_MULTITENANT_SCHOOL_PHASE1 during the tenant-injection test. Cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 multi-tenant unblock for super_admin is fully validated. Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_multitenant_phase1.py covers the full P0 regression and can be re-run on every Phase 2 PR. The only finding is a cosmetic gap: Pydantic School model omits `mantenedora_id` from API responses though the field IS persisted in MongoDB. Future Phase 2 work needs to wire tenant_scope into students/staff/classes/courses/enrollments/grades/learning_objects/etc routers (schools.py is the only one wired today).
rca of the issue: Phase 1 fix verified: auth_middleware.require_roles now fast-paths super_admin (and gerente as admin), check_school_access and get_user_permissions include super_admin/gerente, list endpoints (students, classes, schools, etc.) include super_admin/gerente in wide_roles, and POST /api/schools injects mantenedora_id when X-Mantenedora-Id header is provided. Persistence confirmed via direct MongoDB query. No 403 regressions observed.
